name: Product UX Improvements & UI Redesign Full Scope

subtasks.name: Design System & Core Brand Elements
subtasks.description:
PHASE 4 — Design System & Core Brand Elements
Development and refinement of the core visual system that will shape the overall product experience throughout the redesign process. This phase includes the evolution of brand-related elements, typography decisions, color systems, iconography, visual assets, and scalable UI foundations such as components, tokens, and style structures.

Rather than being handled as a completely isolated production stage, many of these design system decisions and refinements will continue evolving organically throughout the UI design process as the product language, interface needs, and overall visual direction become clearer.

An important part of this phase is directly connected to the overall branding foundation of the product. If there is an existing and actively usable branding direction, visual identity system, or brand guidelines, these tasks will be shaped and supported accordingly. Otherwise, parts of this phase may naturally evolve into a lighter branding-oriented process while the product’s visual language and interface identity are being established. I will also need guidance and additional information from your side regarding the current branding direction, existing assets, and long-term brand expectations throughout this process.

subtasks.name: 4.1 - Wordmark & Emblem Rework [IMPORTANT]
subtasks.description:
This topic is highly important and I will need your guidance and thoughts regarding the brand direction throughout this process.

We need to refine the existing wordmark to achieve a cleaner, more contemporary, and stronger visual identity. The current approach focuses on improving the overall visual quality through typography refinement, balanced proportions, and stronger brand presence rather than conducting a deep full-scale branding process.

Most importantly, the brand currently appears to rely primarily on the existing wordmark, and I was unable to identify an actively used emblem across either the website or the app experience. If there is a specific reason behind this, I would be glad to understand the background and current brand considerations.

From a product and mobile app perspective, I believe the platform would strongly benefit from having a more recognizable brand emblem. A refined or newly developed emblem direction could help establish a more cohesive visual identity across the mobile app, website, and future product ecosystem.

Depending on the importance level of this topic for your brand, I would actually recommend treating this as a more dedicated and foundational design process early in the project timeline. The character, typography, and visual personality of the wordmark and emblem directly influence the overall UI language and design direction that should be built around the product.

At the same time, I understand that you may not currently want to approach this as a separate standalone rebranding project. Because of that, the current task is planned primarily as a refinement and modernization process based on the existing visual materials and overall brand direction.

subtasks.name: 4.2 - App Icon Design
subtasks.description: Adaptation and refinement of the existing FPV wordmark into a mobile app icon format. This task includes creating a simple, clean, and visually strong app icon aligned with the current FPV brand identity. If necessary, the process may include limited visual explorations and iterative refinements to evaluate different app icon directions. However, the current goal is to achieve a simple but polished and effective app icon solution rather than conducting a large-scale icon exploration process.

subtasks.name: 4.3 - Typography solutions
subtasks.description: Typography exploration and refinement for the overall app experience, including researchand selection of suitable typefaces for headlines, base texts, numeric usage,and interface readability. This task also includes defining typography hierarchy, sizing, weights, spacing, and related style guide values to establish a clean, scalable, and visually cohesive typography system throughout the product.

subtasks.name: 4.4 - Preparing Color Pallets
subtasks.description:
Development and refinement of the core product color system, including primary, secondary, accent, and supportive color definitions for the overall UI experience. This task includes building visually balanced and scalable color palettes designed to work harmoniously across different interface areas, components, states, and product contexts.

Throughout the design process, colors may continue evolving and being tested directly within real UI layouts, components, and screens in order to evaluate readability, usability, visual hierarchy, accessibility, and overall product atmosphere before reaching their final refined state.

subtasks.name: 4.5 - Iconography & Creating consistent Icon Set
subtasks.description: Development and refinement of a visually consistent icon system aligned with the overall product style and UI language. This task includes selecting, adapting, and creating iconography solutions that maintain clarity, usability, scalability, and visual consistency across different screens, components, and interface contexts throughout the app.

subtasks.name: 4.6 - Visual Asset Creation & Curation
subtasks.description:
Preparation, selection, refinement, and integration of supporting visual assets such as illustrations, photography, graphical materials, and other interface-related visual elements based on the selected design direction and overall product style.

At the moment, many areas of the app contain noticeable visual repetition, empty spaces, or structurally weak content presentation. Carefully selected and properly integrated visual materials can help create a richer, more balanced, and more engaging product experience while improving overall interface atmosphere and communication quality.

The selected design direction will also play a major role in determining what kinds of visual assets, imagery styles, and supporting materials should become part of the product’s visual language throughout the redesign process.

subtasks.name: 4.7 - Figma Design System Setup — Tokens, Components & Styles
subtasks.description:
Development and organization of the core Figma design system structure, including color tokens, typography scales, spacing systems, grids, elevation styles, reusable UI components, and shared interface foundations.

This task focuses on building a scalable, organized, and reusable design environment that supports visual consistency, faster UI production, and long-term maintainability throughout the redesign process as the product system continues evolving.

subtasks.name: Finalization, Delivery & Extended Support
subtasks.description:
Final refinement, delivery preparation, tablet adaptation review, and extended collaborative support processes following completion of the primary redesign workflow.

This phase focuses on reviewing the product as a complete system after the core redesign process has been completed. Certain tasks within this phase may continue evolving depending on final feedback, usability observations, operational findings, and overall project direction identified toward the later stages of production.

subtasks.name: 7.1 — Overall UI Review, Polish & Refinement Pass
subtasks.description:
Final full-app review process focused on identifying remaining visual inconsistencies, UX friction points, structural weaknesses, interface quality issues, and overall product-level refinement opportunities across the completed redesign system.

This phase may include additional design polishing, usability refinements, hierarchy improvements, consistency corrections, interaction refinements, and overall visual quality improvements discovered after reviewing the product as a more complete ecosystem rather than as isolated screens.

subtasks.name: 7.2 — Responsive Tablet Adaptation (UX, UI)
subtasks.description:
Review and adaptation planning for tablet experiences, including usability considerations, layout behavior, navigation adjustments, and overall interface adaptation across tablet screen sizes.

Depending on the final design direction and workflow structures, certain areas may require dedicated tablet-specific refinements rather than simple responsive scaling solutions.

subtasks.name: 7.3 — Survey Preparation, Workflow Questions & Feedback Structures
subtasks.description:
Preparation of collaborative surveys, structured workflow questions, and feedback-collection systems used throughout the redesign process in order to better understand operational requirements, client expectations, workflow behaviors, usability priorities, and design-direction decisions.
This process may include:
initial project-level surveys,
design-direction questionnaires,
workflow clarification discussions,
page-specific feedback structures,
operational discovery questions,
and task-related collaborative review forms where necessary throughout production.
The answers, notes, and feedback collected throughout these processes will play a critical role in shaping UX decisions, workflow structures, usability improvements, and overall product direction during the redesign process.

subtasks.name: Feedback Form & Survey Questions
subtasks.description:
# Floor Plan Visuals Mobile App Design — Client Feedback & Discovery Survey

This task represents the discovery survey and feedback form prepared to align on branding, design directions, user feedback, and operational priorities. Please review and respond directly to the following questions:

### 1. Brand Assets & Guidelines
Please share your current brand assets/documents, including the currently used wordmark/logo, emblem/symbol, colours, typography, or any existing brand-related materials.

### 2. Branding Rework & Scope Limitations
Before starting the main UX/UI production process, I believe we should first review and rework the currently used wordmark/logo and emblem structure in order to establish a stronger and more consistent visual foundation for the redesign. Do you have any concerns, limitations, or preferences regarding how far the current branding can be updated, modernized, or visually expanded?

### 3. Visual & Aesthetic Preferences
Do you currently have any specific visual style or design direction in mind regarding the redesign or are there any apps, platforms, products, or digital experiences that you personally like, admire, or would want the redesign direction to feel closer to in terms of quality, usability, atmosphere, or overall presentation?

### 4. Competitor Analysis
Could you please share your current competitors in order of importance from your perspective?

### 5. Existing Customer Feedback & Issues
If you currently maintain any list of customer feedback, complaints, recurring requests, or app-related issues collected from users, please share them if possible.

### 6. Team Feedback & Operational Observations
Beyond the previously discussed needs and requests, I would appreciate it if you could internally discuss the app together with your full team and share any additional thoughts, concerns, priorities, or operational observations you collectively have regarding the current experience.

### 7. Critical Sections & Operational Workflows
Which sections, workflows, or operational areas of the app do you consider the most critical, valuable, or important from both business and user perspectives? If possible, please also prioritize them in order of importance.

### 8. Project Reviewers & Approvers
Who will primarily be responsible for reviewing and approving design decisions throughout the project?

subtasks.name: 7.4 — Final Client Presentation: Collaborative Review & Refinements
subtasks.description: Final collaborative review process covering remaining feedback, refinement requests, minor UX improvements, polish iterations, and final adjustment cycles prior to project delivery.

subtasks.name: 7.5 — Figma Project Organization & Delivery
subtasks.description:
Ongoing organization, cleanup, and maintenance of the Figma project structure throughout the entire redesign process, including page organization, naming consistency, component structures, reusable assets, design system management, and overall file readability.

This phase also includes final preparation and structured delivery of the completed Figma project files to ensure the system remains scalable, organized, developer-friendly, and easy to navigate during both production and final handoff.

subtasks.name: Full App Design
subtasks.description:
Full UI design process covering the existing FPV mobile app structure, including screens, sub-pages, modals, forms, and main and sub-navigation components. The current app structure is used as a reference foundation during the redesign process; however, layouts, navigation systems, page structures, flows, and screen organization may continue evolving throughout the UX analysis, solution development, and design production phases.

The subtasks and estimations listed throughout this proposal are primarily based on the current app structure and available screens at the time of evaluation. Depending on product and usability needs, new screens may be introduced, existing screens may be reorganized or divided into multiple parts, and interface structures may be refined to create a more scalable, functional, and improved user experience.

This phase will progress screen-by-screen throughout the production process. The workflow will generally include reviewing the current experience, identifying UX opportunities and structural problems, developing UX and interface solutions, producing updated UI designs, sharing the results for review, collecting revisions and feedback directly within Figma through pin-based comments and discussions, and iteratively refining the designs until satisfying results are achieved.

As the redesign evolves, many areas of the product may continue changing organically through ongoing UX evaluations, design decisions, workflow discoveries, usability considerations, and iterative refinement cycles during production.

subtasks.name: 5.1.1 - Contextual Onboarding Design
subtasks.description:
Design and integration of a contextual onboarding experience presented before authentication screens. This onboarding flow will introduce the app’s core functionality, workflow structure, and overall platform purpose through a step-by-step visual presentation.

Depending on the selected visual direction and overall product style, the experience may include custom illustrations, curated imagery, visual storytelling elements, and supportive interface layouts designed to create a more engaging and welcoming first impression for users.

The onboarding screens will also include primary authentication entry points such as Login and Register actions, allowing users to directly continue into the platform experience.

subtasks.name: 5.2 - Home Page (Dashboard)
subtasks.description:
Main operational dashboard experience covering order summaries, active projects, upcoming appointments, notifications, quick-access actions, promotional content, and key workflow-related interface areas.

The current dashboard structure and available content are used as an initial reference foundation during estimation. However, dashboard organization, navigation structure, widget hierarchy, information architecture, interaction patterns, and overall layout direction may continue evolving throughout the UX analysis and redesign process depending on usability needs, workflow improvements, and overall product direction.

subtasks.name: 5.2.1 -  Dashboard Layout & Operational Structure
subtasks.description:
Design and refinement of the main dashboard layout structure, including information hierarchy, operational widgets, summary areas, welcome sections, active project visibility, promotional content, and workflow-focused interface organization.

This task focuses on improving dashboard clarity, usability, accessibility of key actions, and overall operational experience while exploring alternative layout solutions where necessary.

As this is one of the most critical and frequently used areas of the entire app experience, this phase may involve a higher level of exploration, iteration, experimentation, and layout testing compared to other screens. The survey responses, workflow insights, and operational feedback collected from your side will also play a particularly important role in shaping the final dashboard structure and overall user experience direction.

subtasks.name: 5.2.2 - Orders Overview & Project Tracking Experience
subtasks.description:
Design and restructuring of the orders overview experience, including active orders, pending actions, appointment visibility, status tracking, project progression, and operational workflow visibility across different project states.

As this area represents one of the core operational centers of the app and directly affects the day-to-day workflow experience of users, it will require a particularly careful and product-focused design approach. Additional UX exploration, structural refinement, interaction testing, and iterative improvements may be necessary throughout the redesign process in order to establish a more intuitive, scalable, and operationally efficient experience.

This task may also involve improving status communication systems, expandable information structures, contextual actions, workflow visibility, and overall interaction clarity related to project and order management experiences.

subtasks.name: 5.2.3 - Notifications & Alerts Experience
subtasks.description:
Design and refinement of the notifications and alerts experience, including notification listing, visibility, and overall alert organization. Depending on usability and workflow needs, notification grouping structures may also be explored for areas such as operational alerts, scheduling updates, invoice-related notifications, project completion messages, and other workflow-related system events.

This task may also include read/unread states, notification hierarchy, interaction clarity, and overall notification usability patterns throughout the app experience.

The importance level, structural complexity, and overall direction of this module may further evolve depending on the survey responses, workflow priorities, and operational feedback collected throughout the redesign process. At the current stage, the existing notification structure is being used as the primary reference foundation.

subtasks.name: 5.2.4 - Main Navigation Design
subtasks.description:
Design, restructuring, and refinement of the app’s primary navigation experience throughout the redesign process. This task focuses on improving navigation clarity, operational accessibility, workflow efficiency, and overall usability across the main areas of the application.

Because navigation structure directly affects how users interact with the entire product experience, this phase may involve iterative exploration of different navigation approaches, layout behaviors, interaction patterns, and accessibility solutions before reaching the most effective structure for the app ecosystem.

Depending on UX findings, workflow requirements, survey responses, and usability considerations gathered during production, parts of the navigation system may continue evolving throughout the redesign process.

subtasks.name: 5.3 - Account Page
subtasks.description:
User account and profile management area covering profile controls, order-related access points, preferences, payment management, settings, account-related utilities, and additional user-specific operational tools.

The current account structure is used as the primary reference foundation during estimation; however, parts of the page architecture, module organization, navigation hierarchy, and overall account-related workflows may continue evolving throughout the redesign process depending on usability findings, operational requirements, and overall product direction.

Particular attention will also be given to improving structural clarity between account-related features and operational workflow areas throughout the redesign process.

subtasks.name: 5.3.2 - My Orders
subtasks.description:
Design and restructuring of the orders and project tracking experience, including active projects, completed orders, search systems, order visibility, project progression, and operational workflow management across different project states.

Even during the proposal and evaluation stage, it is already quite clear that this area represents one of the most important operational centers of the entire product experience. Based on the current app structure, this section may potentially require a more dedicated, standalone, and deeply considered product area rather than functioning only as a secondary account-related subsection.

Because order tracking appears to represent one of the primary operational experiences of the product ecosystem, the overall placement, accessibility, navigation hierarchy, and structural organization of this area may require deeper UX evaluation throughout the redesign process.

subtasks.name: 5.3.3 - AutoFill Preferences
subtasks.description:
Although this area may initially appear as a simple settings or form screen, it actually represents a highly operational and workflow-critical preference system directly connected to future order creation flows.

The current structure contains a large amount of configuration-heavy content, repeated operational decisions, deliverable preferences, formatting logic, conditional options, and production-related defaults that may significantly affect the usability and efficiency of future order workflows.

Because of this, the redesign process for this area will likely require careful UX simplification, content grouping, hierarchy restructuring, progressive disclosure solutions, and improved interaction patterns in order to reduce cognitive load and create a more manageable and user-friendly experience.

Particular attention may also be required for:
content density reduction
smarter grouping structures
reusable preference logic
expandable sections
onboarding clarity
conditional visibility patterns
repeat-order usability improvements

To improve workflow efficiency and reduce repetitive manual input, larger form-based and operational data-entry areas throughout the app may also benefit from contextual “Save as AutoFill Preference” actions. This would allow users — particularly real estate professionals handling repeated order workflows — to quickly reuse previously entered information, preferences, and operational settings across future orders and related production flows.

The overall structure and operational behavior of this area may continue evolving throughout the redesign process depending on workflow findings and real usage requirements.

subtasks.name: 5.4 - Create New Order
subtasks.description:
This area represents one of the most critical parts of the overall product experience. The order creation process directly affects usability, operational clarity, user confidence, and the overall perception of the platform.

My primary focus during the redesign of this area will be helping users complete what is currently a highly step-heavy and operationally dense workflow in a clearer, more comfortable, and more manageable way.

The current content and available flows were used as the primary reference during the time estimation process. Based on the existing app structure, several order categories and workflow branches appear to be incomplete, placeholder-based, or not yet fully implemented. Since these flows represent core operational services of the platform, they may require additional UX planning, new screen structures, interaction patterns, and dedicated workflow solutions throughout the redesign process.

Due to the complexity and operational importance of this area, certain sections may require additional UX exploration, alternative structural solutions, iterative testing, and multiple refinement cycles in order to achieve the most effective workflow structure. The answers to the questions that will be asked throughout this process, along with additional notes and feedback from both you and your customers, will be especially critical for this area.

subtasks.name: 5.4.1 - New Order Selection Main Screen
subtasks.description:
During the estimation and structural evaluation process, it became increasingly clear that the current order creation experience would benefit from a more functionally separated and user-friendly grouping structure. For this reason, the redesigned Create New Order experience will likely present workflows under two primary categories: creating new production orders from scratch, and working with existing property-related assets already available within the FPV ecosystem.

This approach aims to reduce complexity, improve clarity, and help users navigate operationally dense workflows in a more intuitive and manageable way. Additionally, when users begin creating a completely new order, the system may also benefit from informing or warning them if an existing plan, survey, or related property asset already exists within the inventory system based on the provided property information.

subtasks.name: 5.4.2 - New Property Orders
subtasks.description:
Order creation workflows focused on producing new property-related deliverables from scratch, including survey-based services, remodeling requests, scan operations, and production-oriented service flows throughout the platform.

These workflows represent operational production processes and may continue evolving throughout the redesign process depending on UX findings, workflow requirements, and service structure refinements.

subtasks.name: 5.4.2.B - (Missing) Marketable Plans for Commercial / Mizxed Use
5.4.2.D - (Missing) Remodeling Plans for Commercial / Mixed Use
5.4.3 - Existing Property Inventory & Reusable Assets
subtasks.description:
Workflows focused on searching, purchasing, reusing, and managing previously created property-related assets within the FPV inventory ecosystem, including existing plans, CAD files, and reusable deliverable packages.

This area may continue evolving throughout the redesign process as inventory structures, reusable asset workflows, and operational usage scenarios become clearer during UX analysis and product refinement.

subtasks.name: 5.4.3.A - (Missing) Purchae and Rebrand Existing FPV Plan
5.4.3.C - Search Inventory & Checkout Flow (3 sub pages)
subtasks.description:
High probability of merging the Search Input and Results experience into a more unified and streamlined workflow rather than separating them into completely different pages.

Search & Results Experience
 Address / Property ID search input combined with filters such as State, City, ZIP Code, Square Footage, and CAD availability. Search results may be displayed within the same flow using property cards containing thumbnail previews, square footage information, CAD badges, pricing, and Select / Checkout actions.
Detail & Checkout Experience
 Multi-step checkout and order completion flow including:
Tab 1 — Account Information
Tab 2 — Order Information
Tab 3 — Final Steps
Tab 4 — Terms & Confirmation
The overall structure, step organization, and workflow architecture may continue evolving throughout the UX and redesign process depending on usability findings and operational requirements.

subtasks.name: 5.5 - About, Catalogs, Pricing & Services
subtasks.description:
Design and restructuring of the informational, service discovery, pricing, and support-related areas throughout the app ecosystem. This phase focuses on improving how users understand available services, production capabilities, pricing structures, operational workflows, and platform-related information during their overall app experience.

Rather than functioning only as isolated informational pages, these areas may evolve into more contextually integrated product-support layers connected directly to operational workflows such as order creation, service selection, onboarding, and customer support experiences.

Throughout this process, the positioning, hierarchy, visibility, and accessibility of informational content may be reconsidered depending on usability findings, workflow requirements, and overall product direction. Your answers to the questions I will be asking, along with additional operational notes and feedback from your side, will also play an important role in defining the most effective structure for these areas.

The overall architecture of this section may continue evolving throughout the redesign process as user behaviors, workflow scenarios, and product priorities become clearer.

subtasks.name: 5.5.1 - About Main Screen
subtasks.description:
Redesign and restructuring of the main About experience as a clearer and more useful informational entry point for users. This screen may function not only as a traditional company information page, but also as a lightweight operational guidance and service discovery layer within the app ecosystem.

The current structure and content organization will be re-evaluated from a UX perspective. Certain sections — particularly Services, pricing-related information, production details, and operational guidance — may benefit from becoming more contextually connected to related workflows or more visible within the primary navigation structure instead of remaining only inside the About section.

For example, users creating new orders may naturally expect to understand:
available services,
deliverable options,
optional add-ons,
and approximate pricing structures
directly within related operational flows. Because of this, informational content hierarchy and contextual visibility may continue evolving throughout the redesign process depending on usability findings and workflow requirements.

subtasks.name: 5.5.2 - Services
subtasks.description:
During the estimation process, it became very clear that this area represents one of the most commercially valuable and visually important sections of the entire platform. However, the current structure feels relatively minimal and visually repetitive despite the importance of the services being presented. Many valuable offerings currently appear inside highly similar layouts, which reduces differentiation, perceived value, and overall presentation quality.

This area may benefit from stronger visual hierarchy, clearer service positioning, more premium presentation structures, and potentially broader visibility within the app ecosystem rather than existing only as a secondary section accessible through the About area.

subtasks.name: 5.5.2.A.1 - Service Category Main Page Template Layout Design
subtasks.description:
Design and restructuring of service-category pages used to organize and present grouped service types such as Marketable, As-Built, 3D & Media, and Add-Ons.
These pages will likely require clearer visual hierarchy, stronger category differentiation, and more engaging browsing structures in order to improve service discovery and help users better understand the scope of available offerings. Depending on the selected design direction, these areas may also benefit from richer visual support such as custom illustrations, photography, visual previews, featured examples, or contextual media elements to create a more premium and informative browsing experience.

Additional attention may also be given to improving:
service grouping clarity,
category navigation,
contextual guidance,
and overall service exploration usability.

subtasks.name: 5.5.2.A.2 - Service Detail Page Template Layout Design
subtasks.description:
Design and refinement of individual service-detail pages used to present specific services, deliverables, use cases, and supporting informational content.
The current detail pages appear highly repetitive and visually limited despite representing highly important operational and commercial offerings of the platform. 

During the redesign process, these pages may evolve into richer presentation experiences supported by stronger layout structures, contextual visuals, production examples, testimonials, previous project references, or supporting media depending on the selected design direction.
Particular attention may be given to helping users more quickly understand:
what the service provides,
when it should be used,
what deliverables are included,
and why the service may be valuable for their specific workflow or property type.

If it would not create any marketing or operational concerns for your side, pricing information related to each service should ideally also be accessible directly within the corresponding service pages in order to provide clearer expectations and support more informed decision-making throughout the user journey.

The goal will be creating a clearer, more premium, and more confidence-building service presentation experience throughout the platform.

subtasks.name: 5.5.3.B - Plans
subtasks.description: State-selectable pricing (Arizona base). B&W and Color price brackets by sq ft: 0-1000sqft 50/00, 1001-2000sqft 00/50, 2001-3000sqft 50/00, 3001-4000sqft 00/50. 4000+ sqft: custom quote required.

subtasks.name: 5.5.3.C - From Existing
subtasks.description: Conversion pricing for old/paper blueprints to layered AutoCAD+PDF. Base flat fee starting at 00. Incremental size brackets and revision review rates.

subtasks.name: 5.5.3.D - Rendering
subtasks.description: Visual rendering prices: 3D Exterior Rendering, 3D Interior Rendering (room/material), Fly-Over Animation Video (aerial), Interior Walk-Thru Animation Video. Custom quote via email CAD/PDF submission note.

subtasks.name: 5.5.3.E - MatterPort
subtasks.description: Base services by sq ft: 0-1000sqft 50, 1001-2000sqft 00, 2001-3000sqft 50, 3001-4000sqft 00, 5000+sqft 50/hr. Add-On services: Outdoor Scans 00/hr, Detached Scan 0, Link Renewal 5/3mo, 2D Plan From Scan 50, 2D Color Plan 00, 3D Color Plan 50, Matterport Video below 1000sqft 25, above 1000sqft +0/1000sqft.

subtasks.name: 5.5.5 - (Not Ready) Live Chat Module Placeholder Screen
Initial Design Direction & Validation
subtasks.description:
Presentation and refinement of the initial UI design direction developed following Phase 1. This phase focuses on applying the selected design approach to key screens and core interface areas, establishing the visual foundation and overall product feel of the app.

The initial design I present may already fully match your expectations. Alternatively, you may prefer to provide revisions and feedback on the proposed visual direction so the design can be further refined and improved throughout the process.

This phase will continue until a satisfying and approved design direction is established, without enforcing a strict iteration limit at this stage.

subtasks.name: 2.1 - Initial Design Proposal
subtasks.description:
Initial application of the selected design direction to key screens such as the Home screen and, where necessary, one or more additional interface areas. This task focuses on presenting the visual style, UI approach, layout structure, navigation behavior, and overall interface feel of the product in order to evaluate whether the proposed direction aligns with your expectations.

Additionally, I may present an alternative design proposal during this phase if the collected data suggests that a different design approach warrants your review and feedback.

subtasks.name: 2.2 - [REVISION 1] Design Feedback, Iterations & Refinements
subtasks.description: Revision, refinement, and iterative improvement process following the initial design presentation. This phase includes visual adjustments, layout refinements, styling updates, and overall interface improvements based on your feedback and expectations until the design direction reaches a satisfying and approved state.

subtasks.name: Iterative Review, Validation & Prototype Support
subtasks.description:
PHASE 6 — Iterative Review, Validation & Prototype Support
Continuous collaborative review, revision, validation, and prototype support processes throughout the redesign workflow.

This phase represents the ongoing feedback and refinement layer of the project rather than a single presentation milestone. Throughout production, completed screens and workflows will be continuously reviewed together through Figma-based discussions, annotations, revisions, and iterative feedback cycles until the desired quality and usability direction are reached.

Certain areas may also require partial interactive prototypes to better communicate navigation behavior, interaction logic, and workflow structures during the redesign process.

Due to the highly iterative and exploratory nature of these tasks, scope and required effort may vary significantly throughout production. Some areas may require only lightweight revisions, while others may evolve into deeper UX exploration, validation, prototyping, and refinement cycles depending on workflow complexity, feedback, and operational findings discovered during the project.
The provided time estimations represent reasonable projections based on the currently available information and product structure at the time of evaluation.

subtasks.name: 6.1 — Iterative Screen Reviews & Feedback Cycles
subtasks.description: Continuous screen-by-screen review, feedback collection, annotation handling, and collaborative refinement processes throughout the redesign workflow using Figma-based revision cycles.

subtasks.name: 6.2 — Partial Interactive Prototype Support
subtasks.description:
Creation of selective interactive prototypes where necessary in order to help communicate navigation flows, interaction behavior, operational logic, and overall user experience throughout the redesign process.

Prototype creation may not be required for every area of the product and will depend on workflow complexity, presentation needs, and validation requirements during production.

subtasks.name: 6.3 — Revision & Refinement Implementation
subtasks.description: Implementation of revisions, UX refinements, structural improvements, visual updates, and interaction adjustments based on ongoing collaborative feedback and iterative review cycles throughout production.

subtasks.name: Overall UX Analysis & Screen Solutions
subtasks.description:
Ongoing UX analysis, structural refinement, and interface usability improvements performed throughout the entire redesign and UI production process.

This phase represents the continuous UX-focused layer of the project rather than a separate one-time analysis stage. The goal is to progressively improve usability, clarity, layout structure, navigation behavior, interaction quality, and overall interface consistency as the product design evolves.

Throughout the design process, I will also be asking screen-specific and workflow-specific questions regarding the core purpose of pages, component functionality, user expectations, operational requirements, and existing product problems. These discussions will play a critical role in restructuring screens, improving UX decisions, and building more effective interface solutions during the redesign process.

Due to the iterative and ongoing nature of these UX-focused processes throughout the redesign workflow, estimation is handled collectively at the phase level rather than as isolated screen tasks.

subtasks.name: 3.1 — Screen Structure & Layout Analysis
subtasks.description: Analysis and refinement of screen structures, content hierarchy, spacing logic, and layout organization on a screen-by-screen basis. This task focuses on improving usability, interface clarity, structural consistency, and visual balance across different areas of the product.

subtasks.name: 3.2 — Navigation & User Flow Improvements
subtasks.description:
Ongoing analysis and refinement of navigation systems, operational flows, user paths, and interaction structures throughout the redesign process. This task focuses on improving usability, reducing friction, and creating clearer and more efficient navigation experiences as different parts of the app evolve during UI production.

Before and throughout the design production process, broader changes to page structures, navigation logic, screen organization, and overall application flows may also be proposed where necessary in order to improve usability, scalability, and overall product experience.

subtasks.name: 3.3 — Component & Interaction Solutions
subtasks.description: Screen-by-screen exploration and refinement of component behaviors, interaction patterns, contextual actions, and interface usability solutions throughout the design process. This task includes developing more functional, scalable, and user-friendly UI interaction approaches during ongoing UI production and refinement stages.

subtasks.name: 3.4 — Iterative UX Improvements & Refinements
subtasks.description: Ongoing iterative UX review and refinement process performed throughout the redesign workflow as screens, layouts, flows, and interface structures continue evolving during production. This task represents the continuous back-and-forth UX layer of the project, including ongoing evaluations, usability reconsiderations, structural refinements, and progressive improvements applied as new design decisions, feedback, and product requirements emerge throughout the process.

subtasks.name: Project Scope, Unknowns & Workflow Evolution Note
subtasks.description:
Throughout the estimation and structural review process, several areas of the current app appeared partially undefined, incomplete, placeholder-based, or operationally unclear. Because of this, certain workflows, screens, interaction structures, or service-related requirements may naturally evolve further once the redesign process begins and deeper product discussions take place.

Additionally, as the project progresses, new operational needs, structural changes, feature additions, workflow expansions, or more radical UX/UI direction changes may also emerge based on ongoing discoveries, collaborative discussions, and evolving product goals.

Whenever possible, I will do my best to help accommodate and refine these areas within the existing project structure and allocated workflow. However, if certain requests evolve into substantially larger production tasks, new feature systems, or major structural expansions beyond the currently evaluated scope, additional estimation and scope adjustments may naturally become necessary later in the process.

The current estimations are therefore based on the presently visible app structure, available screens, existing workflows, and the information accessible during the evaluation stage.

subtasks.name: Research & Direction
subtasks.description: Initial research, visual exploration, and direction-setting phase focused on establishing the overall UX/UI foundation and creative approach for the project before entering the main design production process.

subtasks.name: 1.1 - Sector & Competitor Research
subtasks.description: Initial visual and UX research focused on competitor products, industry standards, interface trends, and relevant app experiences before starting the design process. This task is intended to support internal design direction, inspiration gathering, and overall product positioning rather than producing a formal research presentation or deep analytical report.

subtasks.name: 1.2 - Mood Board & Visual Direction
subtasks.description: Visual exploration and creative direction development based on the research phase and overall product goals. This process may include mood boards, style references, interface experiments, and early visual direction studies to help establish a suitable design language for the product. Depending on the workflow and project needs, selected visual directions or references may be shared during the process when necessary.

subtasks.name: 1.3 - Initial Design Concept Definition
subtasks.description: Selection and refinement of an initial visual direction based on research, explorations, and early design experiments conducted during the first stages of the project. This task focuses on narrowing down the overall design direction and establishing a clearer foundation for the upcoming UI design process before moving into the initial design proposal phase.
